component.name: Pelican
component.summary: Sync your music to a Garmin watch on Linux. No telemetry, no cloud.

description.p: Pelican is a Linux-first tool for moving your own music files onto a Garmin watch over USB. Drop a folder of MP3s or FLACs, and the tool transcodes, tags, and uploads — without going through Garmin Express, iTunes, or any cloud service.
Privacy-first by design. The binary makes zero network connections. Your music stays on your computer; your fitness data stays on your watch. Nothing leaves either unless you decide.
A Krypteia tool — part of an effort to give people open-source alternatives to subscription-only platforms.

ul.li: Drag-and-drop sync from your file manager
Auto-transcode FLAC, OGG, Opus, M4A, and more to Garmin-compatible MP3
Local playlists (named track groups you can mass-upload)
Single static binary; no webview, no daemon, no telemetry
ffmpeg-based audio normalization to Garmin-compatible MP3
